regime_fall.current_status: Iran remains in acute crisis as ceasefire extension masks fundamental deadlock: Tehran refuses negotiations under US blockade while seizing ships and mobilizing militia support for internal suppression, while the US escalates sanctions and military positioning. Multiple indicators of regime stress—internal defections, internet blackouts, economic collapse, and fabricated public support claims—compound external military and economic pressure.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 22-23, 2026, Iran seized two cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz, the US Treasury sanctioned 14 entities supporting Iranian weapons programs, Trump extended the ceasefire while maintaining the port blockade, Iranian Parliament Speaker Qalibaf declared reopening the Strait 'impossible' under blockade conditions, and US halted $500 million in dollar shipments to Iraq to counter Iran-backed militias.
